methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

data.narrative:
Perigee Aerospace is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $56.64M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Astranis, Apex Space, Stoke Space) and a broader public market anchor of 5 Space & Aerospace traded companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression approach uses total capital raised as a proxy base and applies a typical Series D step-up multiplier of 1.5x, implying a valuation of $85M. The Peer-Median method references the three private comparables, which trade at a median valuation of $1.0B with a range of $1.0B to $3.5B, directly implying $1.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales was inapplicable due to absence of Perigee's revenue data, though the sector median P/S of 64.2x across public peers suggests significant scale expectations within the sector.

The triangulated analysis across these two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $72M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $542M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Perigee's revenue trajectory and operational maturity relative to peers. The Stage-Progression method suggests a more conservative floor, while the Peer-Median method indicates significant upside if Perigee matches peer-stage performance. Resolution of this range requires visibility into revenue run-rate and customer traction relative to named comparables.
